exemplar.title: To what extent does sex influence the presentation and recognition of autism spectrum disorder, leading to the underdiagnosis of this disorder in women?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does sex influence the presentation and recognition of autism spectrum disorder, leading to the underdiagnosis of this disorder in women?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused, and effectively threaded through section headings and the conclusion.
Topic communication is accurate and sustained, with a precise title and succinct thesis statement.
Methodology describes evaluation of source credibility and validity, showing some informed selection.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks detail on search protocols, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and systematic selection strategy.
The phrasing âto what extentâ¦â implies quantitative assessment not fully matched by the qualitative approach.
A few incomplete sentences disrupt flow, indicating minor lapses in articulation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates accurate and sustained focus with a clear, appropriately connected research question, meriting upper-middle performance. However, methodological description is only âmostly completeââcritical details of source selection and search strategy are missingâso the work aligns with the 3â4 band rather than the top. A score of 4 on a 6-point scale reflects strong communication and focus, offset by method limitations.

strengths: Accurate definitions of ASD, masking, RRBs, and diagnostic concepts from DSM-5.
Consistent use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., meta-analysis, systematic review, camouflaging).
Relevant and up-to-date statistics (CDC, NIMH) ground the discussion in current data.
weaknesses: Occasional drift into descriptive summaries rather than synthesis reduces depth of understanding.
Biological and developmental explanations of ASD are mentioned only briefly, limiting breadth.
Some in-text citations are missing or inconsistent, affecting clarity of source application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows good subject knowledge and generally accurate terminology, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, extended descriptive passages and limited conceptual integration prevent it reaching excellence (5â6). A score of 3 reflects solid understanding with room to deepen synthesis and broaden conceptual coverage.

strengths: Research sources are relevant and appropriately critiqued for sample size, validity, and bias.
Analysis of methodological strengths and limitations (e.g., narrative review bias, social desirability) demonstrates critical engagement.
Argument structure is logical, moving from prevalence to mechanisms and revisiting themes in the conclusion.
weaknesses: Statistical results are reported but not interrogated (e.g., effect sizes, confidence intervals).
Synthesis remains at paragraph level with limited integration into a unified evaluative model.
Alternative explanations or cultural/contextual counterpoints receive minimal critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work offers clear analysis with mostly supported conclusions and reasoned argumentation, aligning with the 4â6 band. While research is mostly relevant and evaluation present, the depth and integration fall short of the top band (7â9). A mid-band score of 4 (out of 12) appropriately rewards solid critical thinking while noting gaps in synthesis and deeper evaluation.

strengths: Consistent application of IB convention: word count, title page, table of contents, sequential headings, and numbered pages.
Layout and formatting follow IB EE guidelines, with MLA citations applied uniformly.
Headings and section signposts (e.g., âSymptom Masking,â âDiagnostic Overshadowingâ) support reader navigation.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in heading hierarchy (e.g., jump to h4 level) disrupt visual flow.
Some spacing and indentation issues in the table of contents reduce visual clarity.
No summary tables or models to aid comparison of conflicting findings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets IB requirements, with all structural and formatting conventions in place. Minor typographical and hierarchy issues do not detract significantly from readability. A top-band score of 4 reflects excellent structure and layout, with only small refinements suggested.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay employs DSM-5 terminology, research-method terms (meta-analysis, p-value, systematic review), and disorder-specific language (RRBs, camouflaging, empathy quotient) accurately and consistently.  Misuse or lay phrasing is rare, placing performance at the top band, though not exemplary enough to note unique conceptual insight.

Word count is given (3 992), the investigation title appears on the first page, tables/section headings are formatted consistently, and the table of contents links to page numbers.  Citations are uniformly in MLA style and separators suggest pages are numbered.  Minor typographical spacing issues do not detract from overall accurate layout, so the highest band is justified.

**Knowledge**: Definitions of ASD, masking, RRBs, diagnostic overshadowing, and key DSM-5 criteria are accurate.  Studies are summarised correctly and contextualised within psychological concepts (e.g., empathy, social desirability bias).  
**Application**: Sources are largely relevant and integrated, yet treatment sometimes drifts into lengthy description rather than synthesis, and biological or developmental explanations of ASD are touched only briefly.  Overall knowledge is good rather than excellent.

**Evidence of method**: The student describes using a variety of peer-reviewed studies, meta-analyses and reviews, and evaluates each for credibility, sample size, and validity.  
**Gaps**: There is no explanation of how sources were located or selected beyond general statements; no inclusion/exclusion criteria, search strategy, or acknowledgement of potential publication bias.  Because methodology is only âmostly completeâ and selection appears partly informed rather than systematic, the work fits the middle band.

**Strengths**: The title is precise and the opening paragraph immediately restates it as a focused research question.  The purpose â to investigate how sex-based differences affect recognition of ASD â is reiterated throughout section headings and in the conclusion, showing sustained focus.  
**Weaknesses**: While clear, the phrasing of the question (âto what extentâ¦â) invites quantitative judgement that the essay ultimately treats qualitatively; the mismatch slightly blurs the intended scope.  Overall, the topic and focus are conveyed accurately and consistently, warranting the upper-band score but not the maximum.

**Breadth and relevance**: The student cites recent peer-reviewed journals, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and authoritative bodies (CDC, NIMH). All material directly concerns sex differences and ASD diagnosis, showing clear relevance.  
**Shortcomings**: Source pool leans heavily on secondary reviews; primary empirical data are fewer and culturally limited, slightly reducing excellence.  Thus the lower end of the 7â9 band is appropriate.

**Strengths**: The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and repeated in the introduction.  It is narrow enough to guide source selection (studies on prevalence, masking, socialisation, and diagnostic overshadowing) and these themes map directly onto the body sections.  
**Limitations**: The questionâs wording implies causality and magnitude yet the method is purely literature review, so âextentâ is not operationalised with clear criteria.  Despite this, the question remains clearly focused and strongly connected to subsequent discussion, meriting a high band score.

The work follows the IB EE convention: title page, abstract elements, table of contents, sequentially headed sections, conclusion, and reference list. Logical progression is easy to follow and no components are missing, fulfilling the top presentation band.

**Argument structure**: Sections build logically from prevalence to mechanisms, and the conclusion revisits each strand to answer the question, indicating a reasoned line of argument.  Evaluations of source strengths/limitations are present, showing some critical stance.  
**Areas for improvement**: The essay rarely weighs competing explanations or alternative viewpoints, and âextentâ is not operationally concluded (no quantitative synthesis).  Evaluation is therefore partially critical rather than fully critical, placing it mid-band.

**Analysis undertaken**: Each study is followed by consideration of sample size, methodology, validity, and implications for the research question.  Comparative reasoning (e.g., conflicting EQ vs SFS outcomes) shows engagement beyond summary.  
**Depth limits**: Statistical results are reported but rarely interrogated (effect sizes, confidence intervals).  Synthesis across themes is limited to paragraph-level statements rather than an integrated model.  The analysis is therefore âclear and mostly supportedâ, matching the mid-band score.

annotations.comment: The title is precise and clearly conveys the research focus on underdiagnosis in women, establishing strong alignment with the research question.
Synthesis of age and IQ subgroup results is clear and logical, supporting the main argument.
Good analytical balance of strengths and limitations in the qualitative Bargiela et al. study, enhancing critical evaluation.
Transition into âSymptom Maskingâ is well signposted, maintaining coherence of argument flow.
The systematic review critique mentions date range but omits description of search protocol. Add details on database selection and keywords.
Statistical results (means, SDs) are presented accurately; discussing significance or p-values would deepen the analysis.
Integration of conflicting EQ, FQ, and SFS findings would benefit from a summary table or conceptual model.
Discussion of social function measures notes conflict accurately and highlights area needing further synthesis.
Identification of sample size disparity and social desirability bias demonstrates thorough critical evaluation.
The âDiagnostic Overshadowingâ heading clearly signals a new theme, supporting reader orientation.
Analysis of anxietyâs symptom overlap with ASD provides nuanced insight into diagnostic challenges.
The wording of âto what extentâ¦â implies quantitative measurement, yet the methodology is qualitative. Consider rephrasing to better match the literatureâreview approach.
Including word count and candidate code demonstrates adherence to IB formatting requirements.
The table of contents is clearly laid out and links headings to page numbers, aiding navigation.
Consider indenting subâsection entries in the contents to visually distinguish hierarchy and improve clarity.
Good use of recent CDC statistics to ground the introduction in upâtoâdate prevalence data.
Definition of ASD from a reputable source (NIMH) establishes solid subject knowledge and frames key concepts.
The final sentence on this page is incomplete, disrupting flow. Ensure all introductory points are fully articulated.
The thesis statement succinctly outlines the argument and directly connects sources to themes, demonstrating clear focus.
The âDiagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorderâ heading is clearly signposted and appropriately leveled.
Citation of NIMH evaluation steps lacks specific inâtext reference tags. Include inline citations for clarity.
Accurate summary of DSM-5 criteria demonstrates strong understanding of diagnostic concepts.
Linking DSM-5 gender diagnostic issues to the literature sets up clear relevance for later analysis.
Clear section heading for âPrevalenceâ aids structure and signposts content effectively.
Discussing a single prevalence ratio invites cultural bias. Consider contrasting with data from non-Western contexts.
The Loomes et al. meta-analysis summary is thorough but omits details on search strategy and inclusion criteria.
Authorship credentials of Loomes et al. are highlighted to justify source credibility effectively.
Inline math uses plain text. Consider formatting inequalities with $$IQ \ge 70$$ for consistency and clarity.
Heading level for âSymptom Presentationâ jumps to h4, breaking hierarchy. Use consistent levels (e.g., h3).
Reporting p-values without effect sizes limits interpretive depth. Include Cohenâs d or confidence intervals.
Linking higher ED prevalence in women to ASD overshadowing is compelling; include specific prevalence percentages of ASD in ED populations.
Acknowledgement of narrative review bias in Cook et al. demonstrates mature critical awareness of source limitations.
Conclusion restates findings but lacks explicit quantification of âextentâ. Consider adding a clearer summary of degree (e.g., moderate, significant).
